notes: Problem templates. The engine instantiates these per region according to that region's problem_palette_weights and the current escalation clock. Each template has a problem_type (matched against agent specialty_problem_types), a base_severity, a tick_per_day (how fast it compounds untreated), an effort_to_resolve (agent-days needed), and a per-template list of effects that fire at thresholds.

templates.title: Memorial Grief
templates.flavor: The Sinkhole anniversary is in mid-July; the riverboat anniversary is in late August. The grief in Graustark is a thing the nodes carry. Untreated, it bleeds into family_succession problems.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: Grief metastasised into the storefront's succession. The son's posts grow shorter.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Someone the family already trusts — or someone who's earned the right to be there without asking. A demon sent to a grief problem will resolve it faster but the grief will be heavier on them when they return.

This is a multi-stage mission — your agent will check in across three afternoons. Each check-in is a choice.

stages.title: the cemetery, Tuesday afternoon
stages.body:
{agent} parked at the gate at 2:42 PM. Sammy is at Pop's marker. He has been there since 1:18. He is holding the flowers Mom cut from the back garden — still wrapped in the kitchen towel Mom uses for flowers. The towel is the towel Pop liked.

Sammy has not looked up. {agent} can see him from the gate. The gate squeaks. The gate has squeaked since 1972.

choices.label: walk in. don't say anything yet.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · slow, attentive · no cost

choices.label: wait at the gate. let him come to you.
choices.summary: +0.5 effort · respects his timing · slower close

choices.label: call Mom from the car phone first.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · brings the family in · −1 cover (billed line)

effects.reason: the car phone call to Mom went on a billed line

stages.title: the sit-with
stages.body:
Sammy is now sitting on the grass next to the marker. {agent} is two paces back. The wind is from the southwest. The flowers are still in the towel.

This is the part that matters. Sammy has not been ready to talk in three Tuesdays. He looks ready today. Or close.

choices.label: sit with him. the whole afternoon if it takes.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · stays late · canon var locks (third Sunday held)

choices.label: tell him a story about Pop. the boat story from '79.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the boat story · Sammy carries it home heavier (W3 burn)

effects.reason: the boat story landed harder than the agent expected

choices.label: ask him about the new chip on the marker's corner.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · practical · opens a thread about the marker's care

stages.title: the drive home
stages.body:
5:18 PM. The sun is on the back of the truck. Sammy is in the passenger seat. The flowers are at the marker; the towel is folded on the dash.

The storefront is twelve minutes by the river road, eight by Ninth. Mom is at the storefront either way. Sammy hasn't said where he wants to go.

choices.label: take Ninth. Mom is at the storefront and the day is the day.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · directly to Mom · the family closes the loop

choices.label: take the river road. let him watch the water.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · slow road · Sammy carries the day home in his own time

choices.label: ask him where he wants to go.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · gives him the choice · what he says becomes a canon fact

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} sat the third Sunday with Sammy. Pop's marker got fresh flowers. Mom waved from the gate at five.
The candles at the riverboat memorial were lit by hands that knew the order. The wax pooled correctly. The grief did not metastasise this week.
{agent} brought the right silence to the right room. T. did not have to say thank you. T. did not have to say anything.
The casserole made it to the right house. The right house was not the house the casserole's note said. {agent} knew which house was the right house.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} brought the wrong flowers. Mom kept them anyway. Mom always keeps them. The grief is heavier because Mom is the one who's keeping.
The cemetery was empty Tuesday. Sammy did not come. {agent} did not wait. Sammy came at five and the cemetery was empty for him too.
The right silence was not at the right room when {agent} got there. The room is the room it was last summer. Worse.

templates.title: Family Succession
templates.flavor: Who runs what after Dante. The storefront's articles of incorporation. The boat's memorial fund. The cathedral's lease. Untreated, the storefront loses its insurance renewal.

if_unresolved_at_severity_8.text: Insurance lapsed at the storefront. Closed for three weeks while it sorts out.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Whoever has standing at the D'Ambrosio table. Sending the wrong person is not a failure — it's a different choice. The family will absorb it.

This is a multi-stage mission — three afternoons at the kitchen table.

stages.title: the storefront's articles of incorporation
stages.body:
{agent} sat at the kitchen table at 2:18 PM. T. brought the file from the storefront's back office in a manila folder labeled INCORP-92 in Mom's handwriting. The articles are typed on a Selectric. Pop's signature is on page three. The signature line for the surviving heir is blank.

T. says the surviving heir is the surviving heir. Mom says the surviving heir is the surviving son. They are saying two different things.

choices.label: sign 'the surviving son' on the line. T.'s name in full.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · canon: the storefront passes to T. · Mom may carry it heavy

effects.reason: Mom carried the signing home heavier than the signing was

choices.label: sign 'the surviving heir' — leave it open. let next month decide.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · postpones the canon · no immediate cost

choices.label: call WIRE_MOTHER on the kitchen phone before signing.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the matriarch advises · costs 1 cover (long-distance call routed through the storefront line)

effects.reason: long-distance call to Lubbock on the storefront's billed line

stages.title: the boat's memorial fund
stages.body:
The memorial fund's check ledger is on the kitchen table at 4:42 PM. Mom has been writing checks from the fund since 1994 to the families of the boat's crew. The balance is $11,840.62. The annual contribution this October would bring it under $10,000 for the first time since the fund opened.

T. wants to honor October. Mom wants the fund to stay above $10K through Aria's eighteenth birthday in 2000. {agent} is at the table between them.

choices.label: honor October at full · the families will read the envelope.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the families get the canonical contribution · the fund dips under $10K

choices.label: half · pace the giving across two seasons.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · fund preserved · families get less but get more later

choices.label: read the back-of-the-bar thread first.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the surviving son's old posts about the fund · only legible if you've been listening

stages.title: the cathedral's lease
stages.body:
Day three, 11:14 AM. The cathedral office. The lease renewal is on the desk. The parish board needs the storefront to be the storefront when the lease lands on their Tuesday meeting. The storefront's articles + the memorial fund are the two things the parish board reads first.

The lease is yours to sign — but the storefront's standing as an entity is what the parish reads.

choices.label: sign · the storefront stands, the cathedral keeps the lease.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the canonical good outcome · the family + parish + cathedral all hold

choices.label: table it · ask for thirty more days.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · postponement · the parish board notes the delay

effects.reason: the parish board reads the delay as ambivalence

resolution_flavor_success: The articles got signed at the right kitchen table. {agent} brought the right pen. The pen was Pop's.
The succession plan landed in the file cabinet. Hector's boy's name is on the second line in pencil. The pencil will be in pen by Labor Day.
resolution_flavor_failure: The wrong name went on the second line. {agent} did not know the right name. The family will have to undo it Tuesday.

templates.title: Lease and Licensing
templates.flavor: The cathedral's variance. The storefront's beer-and-wine renewal. The model home's certificate of occupancy. The Harmony Creek HOA is doing exactly what the HOA is supposed to do.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Paperwork wants paperwork hands. A demon can do it but the demon walks home heavier. A human can do it but the human is the one whose name goes on the form. Pick the cost.

This is a multi-stage mission — clerk visit, paperwork, signatures.

stages.title: the records-desk visit
stages.body:
{agent} parked at the records desk at 10:18 AM. The clerk at window two is the woman who's been at window two since '88. She knows who you are; she does not look up. The form is the form. The variance / renewal / occupancy / whatever-you-came-for is in the drawer behind her.

How {agent} stands at the counter changes which drawer she opens.

choices.label: ask plainly · the form you came for.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · she opens the right drawer · no cost

choices.label: small-talk first · ask about her grandkids.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the clerk sets the form aside for tomorrow · no cost

choices.label: drop a name · invoke the cathedral lease.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · she opens the drawer faster · -1 cover (the name moves through the office gossip-by-noon)

effects.reason: name dropped at the records desk; the office gossip carries it

stages.title: whose name goes on the form
stages.body:
The form is on the desk. Three signature lines. One is the agent's; one is the supervisor's (the supervisor signs off the day's variances at lunch); the third is the witness.

The witness can be the clerk, the agent's own emergency contact, or someone else from the office. Each choice routes the form differently.

choices.label: let the clerk witness · simple, in-house.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · stays in the building · the variance is filed by 5 PM

choices.label: your own emergency contact · keeps it private.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · slower · the office gossip stays out

choices.label: ask the_rectory_aide to come down · sets up the church-parish goodwill.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · costs nothing now · the aide will note the favor

resolution_flavor_success: The variance got stamped. The clerk was the right clerk. The form went in the right pile, which is the pile the clerk does on Tuesday.
{agent} pulled the file at lunch. The file was there. The renewal was 90 seconds of signatures and the seal had ink.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} got the wrong clerk. The wrong clerk does the right pile but only on Thursday. The form will be Thursday's problem.

templates.title: Infrastructure Failing
templates.flavor: The diner's walk-in. The cathedral's HVAC. The storefront's grease trap. The model home's foundation crack. Things break in old buildings and in new ones.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: The infrastructure failed in a way that's now visible. The node is dark for the week.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Hands. Someone who's worked the building or worked one like it. Send a fast hand and the fix is sloppy; send a careful hand and the fix is slow.

This is a multi-stage mission — the diagnosis, the fix, the follow-up.

stages.title: the diagnosis · what actually broke
stages.body:
{agent} is at the failing thing at 10:42 AM. The presenting symptom is the symptom — the boiler kicks off, the walk-in cycles wrong, the panel trips at noon. The actual cause is not the symptom.

Three paths to diagnose. Each path leads to a different fix in stage 2.

choices.label: trace it. cable by cable, valve by valve.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · careful · finds the real cause · takes the day

choices.label: replace the obvious thing and call it good.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · fast · may have fixed the symptom not the cause · queues a 4-week burn

effects.reason: the obvious thing was the symptom, not the cause

choices.label: read the workshop thread on the cathedral AC swap.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the canon technical knowledge · only legible if you've read THE_WORKSHOP

stages.title: the fix · with what you have on the truck
stages.body:
Whatever {agent} brought is what's on the truck. The parts in the toolbox are the parts in the toolbox. The right part is at the supply shop in Mobile and the supply shop in Mobile is closed until Tuesday.

Do it now with what's here, or wait until Tuesday.

choices.label: now · with the part on the truck. it'll hold.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · resolves the problem · slight chance of a re-fail later

choices.label: wait until Tuesday · right part, right install.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · postponed fix · the problem ages a week

choices.label: improvise with the matriarch's binding-code wisdom.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the right work the wrong way · costs nothing now but JF will note

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} found the loose connection at 4:18 PM. The fix took 22 minutes. The boiler is humming the right semitone again.
The conduit work {agent} did in '91 was the conduit work that needed checking. They checked it. It held.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} fixed the wrong thing. The wrong thing was a real thing that needed fixing. The right thing is still wrong. Two visits Saturday.

templates.title: Cathedral Visitor
templates.flavor: Someone is in the warehouse-cathedral who shouldn't be. Most of the time it's a kid who walked in through the back; sometimes it's somebody worse.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint:
Quiet eyes. Someone who can read a room without being read. A demon assigned to a watch can read it deeper but if they're spotted the cover damage is real.

This is a multi-stage mission — the watch, the read, the decision.

stages.title: the watch · 8 to 11 in the back pew
stages.body:
{agent} took the last pew at 7:48 PM. The 8 AM rehearsal is in the choir loft. The visitor — the person who shouldn't be in the warehouse-cathedral — is at the chapel of repose, just out of sight from the back pew. Three hours of watching.

The visitor is doing the visitor's thing. Whatever that is, it's quiet.

choices.label: watch from the pew. note who they look at.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · careful surveillance · low risk of being read

choices.label: walk past the chapel of repose at 9:42 PM · close read.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · gets the canon read · slight cover risk

effects.reason: agent's close-read pass past the visitor

stages.title: the read · who they are, what they came for
stages.body: By midnight {agent} has the read. The visitor came for one of three reasons. The reason determines the response. There is no fourth reason.

choices.label: a kid · they came in through the back · feed them, send them home.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · clean resolution · sets a flag the cathedral notes

choices.label: a journalist · politely indicate they should leave.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · resolution + cover cost · they won't be back tonight

effects.reason: the journalist will write down how they were asked to leave

choices.label: someone else's agent · they were sent here on purpose.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · this is the worst read · queues a follow-up · sets a watch flag

effects.reason: the agent reports back; the enemy now knows what time the chapel is empty

choices.label: ask the river-house thread first · is this on their schedule?
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the hidden-board intel · only legible if you've dialled the river house

resolution_flavor_success: {agent} sat in the last pew from 8 to 11. The visitor came at 9:42. {agent} knew the visitor's father. The visitor left with a cup of coffee and a name to call.
It was a kid. It is always a kid. {agent} gave the kid a sandwich and the bus number that goes back to the kid's grandmother.
resolution_flavor_failure: {agent} was made. The visitor was not a visitor. The visitor was a reporter. The cathedral keeps a record now.

templates.title: Diner Threshold
templates.flavor: Someone is sitting at booth six who shouldn't be. The threshold the diner maintains has had a soft spot all summer.

templates.title: Surveillance Posture
templates.flavor: Cameras at the gate. License plate readers at the entrance. The HOA's new security committee is meeting every Tuesday. The posture is the thing — the cameras are the symptom.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The cul-de-sac is now a surveilled space. Future operations there cost +50%.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Eyes that already know the road. Someone who can read camera angles without being read by them. This is a multi-stage mission — the survey, the committee meeting, the posture move.

stages.title: walking the gate · counting the lenses
stages.body:
{agent} walked the south gate at 7:48 AM. Six cameras visible · three are recent (matte-black, new bracket mounts) and three were already here (cream-colored, sun-faded). The plate reader is a 1996 Genetec model · the same one Mobile installed last fall.

The survey takes the form the survey takes.

choices.label: count + map · build the kit-list.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · clean info · no flags raised

choices.label: follow one of the cameras' cable run back to its source.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · finds the head-end · 1 cover (you were on someone's tape for that walk)

effects.reason: agent on tape walking the cable run

stages.title: the Tuesday security-committee meeting
stages.body:
The HOA's new security committee meets Tuesday at 7 PM in the clubhouse. Three voting members + Mrs Salinas as the secretary (she takes minutes; the minutes go in the records desk by Wednesday). Tonight: the camera-installation budget for August.

How {agent} reads the room determines what gets in the minutes.

choices.label: sit in the back · listen, don't comment.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · the minutes go in as filed · no cover risk

choices.label: raise the Genetec system's '95 false-flag rate.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · plants doubt in the committee · costs 1 cover (you're now on their roster)

effects.reason: agent on the HOA security-committee meeting roster

choices.label: talk to Mrs Salinas after · the minutes are flexible.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the minutes go in soft · requires having read the W6 HC kitchen thread

templates.title: HOA Action
templates.flavor: A specific ordinance, a specific fine, a specific notice nailed to a specific door. The HOA does this in writing and the writing is legible later.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Someone who reads ordinances for fun. The HOA's writing is the kind of writing that wins on its own terms — match it. This is a multi-stage mission — read the notice, find the precedent, file the response.

stages.title: the notice on the door
stages.body:
{agent} pulled the notice off the door at 9:42 AM. Three paragraphs, Tahoma 11, double-spaced. The ordinance cited is Section 4.7 of the bylaws. The fine is $150 if unanswered in seven days.

The notice has a specific person's name typed at the bottom · either the HOA secretary or one of the committee members. Reading whose name it is tells you what was meant.

choices.label: read the bylaws cited section.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · finds the technical defect · no cost

choices.label: call the name at the bottom directly.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · resolves with conversation · 1 cover (your name is on their list now)

effects.reason: phone call to the HOA notice signer

stages.title: the response
stages.body: By the end of the day {agent} has a response prepared. The form of the response matters as much as the content. The HOA reads what they like to read.

choices.label: match their format · 3 paragraphs Tahoma 11.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · clean response in their language

choices.label: cite a different ordinance · push back.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · sets up a longer fight · the file goes on the chairman's desk by next Tuesday

effects.reason: the chairman reads pushback as defiance; the next notice will be specific

choices.label: write the response in Mackenzie's own voice.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · disarms the HOA · requires having read her unleashed-on-the-boards thread

templates.title: Missing Kid
templates.flavor: Not yet a national story. Yet. The flier is at the community pool. The mother is in the HOA office at 9 AM Monday and at 9 AM Tuesday and at 9 AM Wednesday.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: It went national. Local coverage is now national coverage. Cover in the region is gone.

templates.title: Community Meeting Goes Wrong
templates.flavor: The Thursday-night HOA meeting. Or the Wednesday-night town hall in Small Wood. The motion was supposed to be the motion. The motion is now something else.

templates.title: Newsletter Item
templates.flavor: Page three of the HOA newsletter, the column the residents read first. The item is named in a way that lets adjacent residents know who it's about.
templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The newsletter is words on paper. Match them with words on paper, or get the words off the paper before it prints. Pick the angle. This is a short two-stage mission · the print deadline is Thursday.

stages.title: the draft on the editor's desk
stages.body:
{agent} got eyes on the draft at 2:18 PM Tuesday. The column on page 3 is 240 words. The item names a 'recent resident' and the adjacent paragraph names the cul-de-sac. The two together don't quite name anyone but they read close enough.

Thursday is the print deadline.

choices.label: ask the editor (Patricia) for a sentence-level edit.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · clean editorial fix · the item still runs but unnamed

choices.label: submit a competing item · push it off page 3.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the column gets bumped · costs no cover

choices.label: give Patricia the Mackenzie 'how-to-write-without-naming' note.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · disarms the whole column for the season · requires reading Mackenzie's W8 letter

templates.title: Model Home Has The Wrong Feel
templates.flavor: The model home is, this Tuesday, slightly cold around the kitchen island. The realtor walking the next family through notices and doesn't, on her way out, say what she noticed.

templates.title: Local Press Exposure
templates.flavor: Page B6 of the Small Wood Gazette. Five paragraphs, three of them about Frasier's contact's brother. The fourth is about the room above the timber yard.

templates.title: Contact Going Dark
templates.flavor: The call back hasn't come. It's Tuesday. By Friday it's a thing.

templates.title: Seed Dying
templates.flavor: A planting attempt that hasn't taken. The room is rented and quiet but no one is going to it. The contact is on the way out. The next plant is going to cost more.

templates.title: Demon Corruption Event
templates.flavor: A demon currently in Small Wood is teetering. The corruption rating is at threshold. The next dispatch decides whether you pull her or push her.

templates.title: The Ground Refuses
templates.flavor: Sometimes Small Wood does not want what Frasier is sending. The dirt road that was supposed to be a node says — through the locals who walk it — no.

templates.title: Town Meeting Pushback
templates.flavor: The Wednesday town hall in Small Wood. The room knows who's new. The room has been the room for fifty years.

templates.title: Turned Demon Active
templates.flavor: A demon Frasier trained is now on the resistance's side. The problems originate from a name on the map that used to be one of his.

templates.title: Wake Problem
templates.flavor: Spawned by Steamboat's failure mode. The wake registered three counties over and the wake is now its own problem.

templates.title: Cathedral Basement Relay
templates.flavor: The W14 storm came north of the keys. The cathedral basement is the inland relay for the gulf-coast circle. JF is on the boiler; the line stays up only as long as someone's at the desk. Cross-BBS coordination through the storm window.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: [color=#ff9090]The relay dropped at 11:48 PM. STEEPLE tried to call back from Mobile and got no carrier.[/color]

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.reason: the relay dropped; the gulf-coast circle holds it against you
